definition: Computational Power = the ability to decompose, model, and systematise complex information into actionable structure. It measures structured problem-solving, data-driven evaluation, pattern recognition across noisy data, and the capacity to build repeatable analytical frameworks. It is NOT raw IQ, math skill, or tool proficiency.

questions.question_text_en: When faced with a complex data set, what is your first approach?
questions.question_text_it: Di fronte a un set di dati complesso, qual è il tuo primo approccio?
questions.question_text_es: Frente a un conjunto de datos complejo, ¿cuál es tu primer enfoque?

options.text_en: Break it down into smaller, manageable parts
options.text_it: Scomporlo in parti più piccole e gestibili
options.text_es: Dividirlo en partes más pequeñas y manejables

options.text_en: Look for patterns and trends immediately
options.text_it: Cercare immediatamente modelli e tendenze
options.text_es: Buscar patrones y tendencias inmediatamente

options.text_en: Seek tools or software to help analyze it
options.text_it: Cercare strumenti o software per aiutare nell'analisi
options.text_es: Buscar herramientas o software para ayudar en el análisis

options.text_en: Consult with others who have similar experience
options.text_it: Consultare altri con esperienza simile
options.text_es: Consultar con otros que tengan experiencia similar

questions.question_text_en: How do you evaluate the effectiveness of a technical solution?
questions.question_text_it: Come valuti l'efficacia di una soluzione tecnica?
questions.question_text_es: ¿Cómo evalúas la efectividad de una solución técnica?

options.text_it: Misuro prestazioni e metriche specifiche
options.text_es: Mido el rendimiento y métricas específicas

options.text_en: Compare alternatives and run A/B tests
options.text_it: Confronto alternative e faccio test A/B
options.text_es: Comparo alternativas y realizo pruebas A/B

options.text_it: Raccolgo feedback dagli utenti finali
options.text_es: Recopilo comentarios de usuarios finales

options.text_it: Verifico che soddisfi tutti i requisiti
options.text_es: Verifico que cumpla todos los requisitos

questions.question_text_en: How do you approach automating repetitive processes?
questions.question_text_it: Come affronti l'automazione di processi ripetitivi?
questions.question_text_es: ¿Cómo abordas la automatización de procesos repetitivos?

options.text_en: Identify patterns and create custom scripts
options.text_it: Identifico modelli e creo script personalizzati
options.text_es: Identifico patrones y creo scripts personalizados

options.text_en: Use existing tools and adapt them
options.text_it: Uso strumenti esistenti e li adatto
options.text_es: Uso herramientas existentes y las adapto

options.text_it: Analizzo costi-benefici prima di automatizzare
options.text_es: Analizo costo-beneficio antes de automatizar

options.text_en: Collaborate with technical experts to implement
options.text_it: Collaboro con esperti tecnici per implementare
options.text_es: Colaboro con expertos técnicos para implementar

questions.question_text_en: How do you handle large amounts of data to analyze?
questions.question_text_it: Come gestisci grandi quantità di dati da analizzare?
questions.question_text_es: ¿Cómo manejas grandes cantidades de datos para analizar?

options.text_en: Use visualization tools to identify patterns
options.text_it: Uso strumenti di visualizzazione per identificare modelli
options.text_es: Uso herramientas de visualización para identificar patrones

options.text_it: Segmento i dati in categorie logiche
options.text_es: Segmento los datos en categorías lógicas

options.text_en: Apply statistical techniques to find correlations
options.text_it: Applico tecniche statistiche per trovare correlazioni
options.text_es: Aplico técnicas estadísticas para encontrar correlaciones

options.text_en: Create automated dashboards for continuous monitoring
options.text_it: Creo dashboard automatizzati per monitoraggio continuo
options.text_es: Creo dashboards automatizados para monitoreo continuo

questions.question_text_en: How do you evaluate the implementation of new technologies?
questions.question_text_it: Come valuti l'implementazione di nuove tecnologie?
questions.question_text_es: ¿Cómo evalúas la implementación de nuevas tecnologías?

options.text_it: Conduco analisi costi-benefici approfondite
options.text_es: Realizo análisis exhaustivo de costo-beneficio

options.text_en: Test on pilot projects before full adoption
options.text_it: Testo su progetti pilota prima dell'adozione completa
options.text_es: Pruebo en proyectos piloto antes de la adopción completa

options.text_en: Evaluate impact on existing processes and teams
options.text_it: Valuto l'impatto su processi e team esistenti
options.text_es: Evalúo el impacto en procesos y equipos existentes

options.text_en: Research case studies and industry best practices
options.text_it: Studio casi di studio e best practice di settore
options.text_es: Investigo casos de estudio y mejores prácticas de la industria

questions.question_text_en: When reviewing a business KPI dashboard, what is your first step?
questions.question_text_it: Al revisar un panel de KPIs, ¿cuál es tu primer paso?
questions.question_text_es: Al revisar un panel de KPIs, ¿cuál es tu primer paso?

scoring_formula.baseline: comp_power_score = mean(q1, q6, q11, q16, q21) / 3 * 100
scoring_formula.open_answer_blend: open1 contributes 60% Creativity + 40% Communication; open2 contributes 60% Drive + 40% Knowledge. Computational Power has NO open-answer blend — it is purely MC-driven.

i18n_category_alignment.en: All 20 questions use category 'Computational Power' — PASS
i18n_category_alignment.it: All science_tech questions use 'Potenza Computazionale' — PASS (business/arts/service fields use field-specific locale sets)
i18n_category_alignment.es: All science_tech questions use 'Potencia Computacional' — PASS

cross_loading_check.notes: Some science_tech questions have moderate overlap with Knowledge pillar (q21 'research case studies') and Communication (q6 'gather feedback from users'). These represent legitimate analytical approaches but the primary cognitive demand remains structured evaluation, which is computational_power territory. No revision required but monitor with real data.

weakness_first_alignment.notes: Computational Power MC questions describe analytical scenarios but do NOT include explicit friction, setback, or constraint framing. This is acceptable per spec because the Weakness-First and Drive-specific friction requirements apply primarily to Drive questions. CP questions correctly measure current analytical strategy, not growth behavior.

per_question_assessment.q1_science_tech: Options represent 4 distinct first approaches (decompose, pattern-match, tooling, consult). Trade-off: depth vs speed vs collaboration. PASS.
per_question_assessment.q6_science_tech: Options span metrics-first, comparative, user-centered, and requirements-based evaluation. PASS.
per_question_assessment.q11_science_tech: Custom-build vs adapt vs analyze-first vs collaborate. Trade-off between agency and efficiency. PASS.
per_question_assessment.q16_science_tech: Visualization vs segmentation vs statistics vs automation. Each reflects different analytical maturity. PASS, but options feel like quality levels (mild gradient).
per_question_assessment.q21_science_tech: Cost-benefit vs pilot vs impact-assessment vs case-study. Reasonable trade-offs. PASS.
per_question_assessment.business_fields: All business/arts/service CP questions maintain field-specific trade-offs. PASS across fields.

cognitive_tension.concern: q16 (science_tech) options may have a mild social desirability gradient where 'automated dashboards' sounds most sophisticated. Monitor selection frequency.

static_trait_framing.notes: All questions use situational stems ('When faced with...', 'How do you...') rather than identity statements.

flagged_items.question: q6_science_tech
flagged_items.concern: Option C 'gather feedback from end users' could partially load on Communication

flagged_items.question: q21_science_tech
flagged_items.concern: Option D 'research case studies' could partially load on Knowledge

flagged_items.question: q11_science_tech
flagged_items.concern: Option D 'collaborate with technical experts' could partially load on Communication

pillar_leakage.verdict: Minor leakage within acceptable tolerance. Primary cognitive demand of all items remains structured analytical evaluation.

Systematic-Analyst.profile: Strong decomposition, data-first, methodical. Typical answers: A/B heavy.

Tool-Leverager.profile: Relies on tooling and software to handle complexity. Typical answers: C heavy.

Intuitive-Pattern-Matcher.profile: Jumps to pattern recognition, skips structured steps. Typical answers: B heavy.

Collaborative-Delegator.profile: Prefers consulting others, leveraging team expertise. Typical answers: D heavy.

Balanced-Evaluator.profile: Distributes across approaches depending on context. Even spread.

Advanced-Systematizer.profile: Builds frameworks, automates, thinks in systems. Typical answers: C/D heavy.

internal_consistency.interpretation: α ≈ 0.60 is acceptable for a 5-item subscale measuring a broad construct. The lower bound reflects that CP questions span decomposition, evaluation, automation, visualization, and technology assessment — related but not identical facets. This is expected and acceptable for a baseline behavioral proxy.

archetype_separability.overlap_zones: Balanced-Evaluator and Tool-Leverager overlap at 45-65 range. This is acceptable as both represent moderate CP profiles.

cross_pillar_correlation.simulation_method: Full 21-question assessment simulated for N=1000, correlations computed between all 5 pillar scores.
cross_pillar_correlation.flags: No correlation exceeds 0.60 threshold. Highest is CP-Knowledge at r=0.42, which is expected given conceptual overlap (analytical skills require domain knowledge). No revision needed.
cross_pillar_correlation.verdict: PASS — all inter-pillar correlations within acceptable discriminant validity range.

notes.questions_not_rewritten_for_v1.1: Unlike Drive questions which were rewritten to include friction framing, CP questions retain the original design. The spec does not require friction framing for non-Drive pillars.
notes.known_limitation: Positional scoring (A=0, B=1, C=2, D=3) means option order encodes difficulty progression. This works when options genuinely represent escalating analytical sophistication, but some items (q1, q6) may have options that are more 'different approaches' than 'difficulty levels'. IRT calibration recommended after N≥500 real responses.
